supply_chain.dependencies: Coal and natural gas supply for generation facilities in Kentucky; reliance on interstate pipelines for natural gas transportation; dependence on transmission operators (PJM, ISO-NE) for grid operations; single-source or concentrated suppliers for coal from specific regions (western Kentucky, southern Indiana, Illinois, West Virginia, Pennsylvania, Wyoming)
risks: Reliability of coal deliveries affected by fluctuations in demand, coal mine production issues, river level events, lock outages, supplier or transporter operating or financial difficulties; natural gas supply disruptions; counterparty performance and credit risk; supply chain disruptions or delays; inflation and supply chain related risks; potential defaults by suppliers requiring replacement power or fuel procurement
Dependence on OVEC power purchase contract through 2040
Supplier default requiring replacement power or fuel in market

regulatory_risks: Clean Air Act compliance requirements
NERC mandatory reliability standards enforced by FERC
DHS Transportation Security Administration cybersecurity directives for natural gas facilities
National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) for ozone and particulate matter
Pennsylvania Alternative Energy Portfolio Standard (AEPS) compliance
Coal combustion residuals (CCR) management regulations
Grid modernization plan approvals
Permit requirements for construction, operation, and environmental compliance
Superfund and other environmental remediation obligations
Federal and state environmental remediation obligations
Cross-State Air Pollution Rule (CSAPR) and Good Neighbor Plan requirements
Waters of the United States (WOTUS) definition changes
EPA environmental regulations including ELGs and CCR rules
Renewable energy standard compliance obligations
Federal and state regulatory commission approval requirements for rates (FERC, KPSC, VSCC, PAPUC, RIPUC)
Rate case proceedings and cost recovery mechanisms
Smart meter deployment regulatory approvals
Tax law changes (federal and state)
Pennsylvania Act 129 energy efficiency and conservation program requirements
State regulatory commission rate approvals (PAPUC, RIPUC, KPSC, VSCC)
Pension and postretirement benefit cost recovery in rates
Effluent Limitation Guidelines (ELG) for water discharges
Challenges to current or future rate requests by federal or state agencies, intervenors, and other parties
Potential new climate disclosure rules from SEC
Potential new tariffs on imported goods
Manufactured gas plant site remediation requirements
Pipeline safety regulations (PHMSA)
Tax law changes impacting deferred taxes and customer rates
Potential fines, penalties, or forfeitures for environmental non-compliance
Mercury and Air Toxics Standards (MATS) rule modifications
FERC rate regulation and transmission cost recovery
Legislative or regulatory actions regarding industry restructuring or customer choice
Extensive federal, state, and local environmental laws and regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, EPA regulations)
Coal Combustion Residuals (CCR) regulations
EPA regulations on greenhouse gas emissions from fossil fuel-fired electric generating units (Section 111 rule)
Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) regulations for natural gas infrastructure
Department of Homeland Security Transportation Security Administration (TSA) security directives for natural gas systems
Compliance with NERC mandatory reliability standards
Superfund and manufactured gas plant site cleanup requirements
Storm cost recovery regulatory treatment
Energy efficiency program requirements (Pennsylvania Act 129)
Clean Water Act jurisdiction over discharges to groundwater
Rhode Island Renewable Energy Standard and Long-term Clean Energy Standard
